title: C extension swallows non-AttributeError exceptions during optional attribute lookups
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

15 call sites in `src/wrapt/_wrappers.c` call `PyErr_Clear()` unconditionally after an attribute lookup, instead of first checking that the pending
exception is an `AttributeError`. The intent at every site is to ignore a missing optional attribute, but the unguarded clear silently swallows any
exception — including `MemoryError`, `KeyboardInterrupt`, `SystemExit`, and user exceptions raised from `__getattribute__`, properties, or descriptors.

The pure-Python implementation in `src/wrapt/wrappers.py` is unaffected — it uses `try/except AttributeError`, `getattr(..., default)`, and `hasattr(...)`, all of which only suppress `AttributeError`.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
